key_insight: In UK Biobank data, plasma proteins such as GDF15 and ACE2 appear to mediate pathways from cardiometabolic disease to later cardiovascular, neurologic, renal, and pulmonary complications.
clinical_relevance: If validated, these mediator proteins could improve risk stratification and suggest new biomarker-guided targets for preventing downstream disease.
method: Mediation analysis across 53,030 participants linked 3 primary cardiometabolic diseases to 18 secondary outcomes.
Mendelian randomization tested whether identified proteins may have causal roles.
Machine learning assessed whether adding mediator proteins improved prediction versus clinical factors alone.
evidence_signal: The study reported 998 significant mediation pathways involving 337 unique proteins, with 44 proteins supported by Mendelian randomization and improved predictive performance when mediators were added to models.
watch_item: These findings are observational and biomarker-based, so clinical utility and therapeutic targeting still require independent replication and prospective validation.
paper_title: Plasma Proteomics Linking Primary and Secondary diseases: Insights into Molecular Mediation from UK Biobank Data
